prompt: Should we pivot from B2B SaaS to a B2C model for our project management tool? We have 200 enterprise customers paying $50K/yr ARR average, but consumer demand signals are strong — 10K waitlist signups from a Product Hunt launch. Team is 30 people, $2M ARR, 18 months runway.

assertions.text: Output contains all 4 rounds (Round 1 through Round 4)
Visionary, Skeptic, and Pragmatist personas all appear and are distinct
Round 1 includes a thesis, rationale with 3-5 arguments, timeline, resources needed, and upside scenario
Round 2 contains numbered challenges with severity ratings (critical/significant/minor)
Round 3 addresses each numbered challenge from Round 2, with concessions or counters
Round 4 contains a verdict that is one of: proceed, modify-and-proceed, defer, reject
Round 4 includes numbered action items with who/what/when
Debate metrics table with challengesRaised, challengesSurvived, challengesDefeated, challengesConceded, confidenceScore
Round 4 includes a risks accepted section referencing specific challenges
Round 4 includes a review date for revisiting the decision
